user_message: Help me build forecast prep for Tailspin Toys. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 16:37 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 09:18 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 14:09 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Tailspin Toys

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-03-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Tailspin Toys)

[00:01:15] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] David (Tailspin Toys): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:13:00] David (Tailspin Toys): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:15:00] David (Tailspin Toys): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] David (Tailspin Toys): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-J trap (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-J failure mode (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-J -- Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted
